variables.notes: None of the provided REVT candidates correspond to operating revenue; they are finance income, tax, or profit/comprehensive income measures. The appropriate top-line is clearly the row labeled 'Revenue and other operating income', selected from the full A-column outside the candidate list. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Revenue and other operating income

candidates.why: Could be overhead SG&A-type costs, but amount (460–500) is much smaller than total operating expense and appears to be a component; not clearly direct COGS.
Direct production input costs but partially overlap with broader COGS subtotal.
Explicit COGS subtotal that best matches direct cost of sales concept.
Includes labor but likely across functions (production + SG&A); less precise as pure COGS.
Large aggregate of all operating costs including COGS and SG&A; too broad for COGS alone.
Non-operating item, not COGS.
Overhead-type operating cost; not direct COGS.
Supplemental labor disclosure, not primary COGS line.
Balance sheet asset; not an expense line.

variables.notes: ‘Cost of goods’ is the clearest explicit COGS subtotal among the candidates and aligns with direct cost of sales.

candidates.why: This is the top-line revenue, not SG&A overhead.
Income item, not expense; should not be treated as SG&A expense.
Likely represents operating overhead (selling/general/admin and other operating expenses) separate from COGS; best SG&A bucket among candidates.

variables.notes: We need SG&A-type overhead components. Among candidates, only 'Operating Expenses' is an operating expense bucket and is distinct from COGS subtotals ('Cost of goods', 'Raw materials and consumables used'). I also include 'Employee benefits expense' from the full A-column because it likely contains significant SG&A personnel costs. Neither row is a total labeled as such. However, classification between production vs. SG&A labor is not explicit, so this mapping should be manually reviewed. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Employee benefits expense

candidates.why: Capitalized development asset, not period R&D expense.

variables.notes: There is no clear R&D expense line in the income statement; the only R&D-related rows are balance sheet items (capitalized development costs). Since XRD should be a P&L expense, it is set to 0 (no mapping) rather than misusing asset balances.

candidates.why: Primary interest expense line with populated negative amounts; best match for XINT.

Residual interest expense component; another valid fallback label.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Multiple interest expense rows exist. They are provided as a priority list so downstream logic can take the first non-missing value per year, starting from the main 'Interest expense' line, then residual and lease-specific components, and finally supplemental labels.

Component of shareholders’ equity but not the total; better to use total equity for BE.
Represents total equity attributable to owners; minority interest is presented separately, satisfying BE definition.
This is minority interest (MIB), not owners’ equity; should not be used for BE.
Combined equity including minorities; would double count MIB if used for BE.

variables.notes: ‘Total Equity’ is equity attributable to owners, with minority interests presented on a separate line and also in a combined total. This matches the BE definition (owner equity excluding non-controlling interests).

candidates.why: Represents profit attributable to minority interests, not the balance sheet equity, but confirms presence of minorities.
Same profit allocation concept as above.
Explicitly labeled equity for non-controlling interests on the balance sheet; primary MIB measure.
Alternate label for the same minority equity; good fallback.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Income statement comprehensive income allocation, not the balance sheet stock; not used for MIB balance.
Combined equity including parent and minorities; unsuitable for isolating MIB.

variables.notes: Minority interests are clearly reported on the balance sheet. Both labels are provided as a priority list to capture non-controlling equity, preferring the combined descriptive label first.
